channel.title: Morning Mail
channel.description: Stay updated on the latest news and events happening in the RPI community.

item.title: NYS Assembly Internship Info Session
item.description: We’re excited to announce that a representative from the New York State Assembly will be visiting campus. Undergraduate and Graduate students are invited to stop by, meet our guest, and learn more about their work serving communities across New York State. The NYS Assembly Internship Program offers undergraduate and graduate students the chance to gain real‑world experience in legislative offices. Stop by on March 17 from 4-5pm in Sage 4304.
item.author: Students

item.title: CRCC Job and Internship Fair
item.description: It’s not too late to secure an internship or expand your full-time search! Attend the Capital Region Career Consortium Job and Internship fair on April 8. Register via Handshake: https://app.joinhandshake.com/career_fairs/905492b0-2207-4be4-abe1-8c8a4d842386/student_preview
item.author: Students

item.title: Success Center Hosting Review Sessions Week of 3/15-3/22
item.description: Week of 3/15- 3/22: Need some last minute review for your upcoming exam? Stop by for a Puck Cookie and a Review Session with our Success Center Tutors! MAU: Sunday, 3/15 (5-7pm) at the Success Center CAL II: Monday, 3/16 (7-9pm) in the Nason Classroom CHEM I: Tuesday, 3/17 (5-6pm) at the Success Center CHEM II: Tuesday, 3/17 (7-8pm) at the Success Center Computer Science I: Tuesday, 3/17 (8-10pm) in the Nason Classroom Diff Eq: Wednesday, 3/18 (7-9pm) at the Success Center
item.author: Students

item.title: Counseling Center Drop-in Counseling Hours for Spring 2026
item.description: The RPI Counseling Center invites students to meet with a counselor without a formal appointment, during designated drop-in hours. There are two hours available for walk-in each day (M/F - 10 a.m. to 12 p.m.; T/W/Th - 2 to 4 p.m.). Academy Hall Suite 4200.
item.author: Students

item.title: Lally AI Academy: Build & Deploy an AI Product in 30 Days
item.description: Expanding your portfolio? Curious about Claude Code? Join a 30-day sprint where your team designs, builds, and deploys a live product using AI and professional-grade tools. Open to all majors, no coding needed. Teams of 3–6 (at least 1 Lally student for commercial orientation, and a faculty/staff mentor) receive up to $500 in AI tools, digital services, and makerspace access. Earn a certificate, practice teamwork, time management, and resource management. Start on or after April 1.
item.author: Students

item.title: Computer Science Thesis Defense - Brandon Rozek
item.description: Date: Wednesday, March 18, 2026 2:00 PM - 3:00 PM - Name: Brandon Rozek Advisor: Selmer Bringsjord Date: Wednesday, March 18th Time: 2 PM Location: Winslow, 4th Floor Virtual Link: https://us02web.zoom.us/j/82547712753?pwd=dn4wqALIgNenCRxnUwlMi1JNsxPwp0.1 Title: Towards Automated Planning and Goal Recognition under Qualitative Uncertainty
item.author: Students

item.title: MS Defense
item.description: Date: Thursday, March 19, 2026 10:00 AM - 12:00 PM - Name: Caleb Carr Advisor: Dr. Chris Sims Day: March 19, 2026 Time: 10:00 am Location: Sage 4112, https://rensselaer.webex.com/meet/carrc4 Title: Disentangling Representation and Policy Complexity in Reinforcement Learning via Dual Mutual Information Penalties
item.author: Students

item.title: Math Colloquium: Dr. Tim Mitchell from CUNY
item.description: Date: Wednesday, March 18, 2026 11:00 AM - 12:00 PM - This Mathematical Sciences Colloquium will feature a presentation titled “Predicting Transient Behavior via Computation of Kreiss Constants” by Dr. Tim Mitchell. He s an Associate Professor of Computer Science at CUNY Queens College and Doctoral Faculty at the CUNY Graduate Center interested in numerical analysis, optimization, and scientific computing.
item.author: Community

item.title: Faculty/Staff Memorial Service to be Held April 14
item.description: In remembrance of the individuals we lost this past year, the Pillars of Rensselaer invite all members of the Rensselaer community, including alumni and friends, to attend this year’s Faculty and Staff Memorial Service Faculty/Staff Memorial Service.
item.author: Community

item.title: A Non-Variational Quantum Approach to Solving Combinatorial Optimization Problems: Iterative-QAOA
item.description: Date: Tuesday, March 17, 2026 4:00 PM - 5:00 PM - Quantum computers introduce new opportunities for modeling and solving decision problems. In this talk, we will first discuss the unique positioning of Industrial Engineering in the quantum research landscape that motivates new directions at the intersection of application and theory. Then as an example, we will present a new algorithm, Iterative-QAOA, for combinational optimization problems.
item.author: Community

item.title: Mare Donohue’s Retirement Celebration
item.description: Date: Friday, March 27th, 2026 12:30-2:30 PM - After nine years of dedicated service to RPI, Mare Donohue, Manager of Client Services Support, will retire at the end of March. As the face of IT, Mare has led the Help Desk with care and commitment, positively impacting countless students, faculty, and staff. Please join us in celebrating her career on Friday, March 27, from 12:30–2:30 PM at the Heffner Alumni House. Kindly RSVP: https://forms.cloud.microsoft/r/W9WxAqbk2j
item.author: Community

item.title: “Maximize Your Summer” with Andrew Morrison ’87
item.description: Date: Monday, March 23, 2026 5:00 AM - 8:00 AM - “Maximize Your Summer” with Andrew Morrison ’87, Turn Your passion & Skills Into Income, Impact, and Opportunity.
item.author: Community

item.title: RPI Orchestra and ESYO Symphony Orchestra Side-by-Side Concert on March 31
item.description: The combined forces of the RPI Orchestra and Empire State Youth Orchestra’s Symphony Orchestra unite to perform Gustav Mahler’s epic Symphony no. 1. The program will be led by RPI music faculty Dr. Robert Whalen, and will also include a performance of Edvard Grieg’s famous Concerto for Piano and Orchestra by 2025 Concerto Competition winner Armstrong Wang ’27. This performance will take place at 7pm on March 31st in the EMPAC Concert Hall. Admission is free.
item.author: Community

item.title: Faculty & Staff Appreciation Lunch – March 18, 2026
item.description: Faculty and staff are invited to enjoy a delicious lunch for just $6 plus tax on Wednesday, March 18, 2026! Join us at Commons or Russell Sage during lunch and we hope to see you there.
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Division of Human Resources Professional Development Live Course: “The Art of Clear and Confident Communication”
item.description: Join us for Dr. Mike Bechtle’s The Art of Clear and Confident Communication Leadercamp and leave empowered with immediately actional tools for communicating effectively in any situation. Two times: 9:00AM - 10:00AM and 12:00PM - 1:00PM.
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Division of Human Resources Professional Development Live Course: “Leading Hybrid Teams That Actually Work”
item.description: Hybrid work is no longer a policy decision—it’s a leadership capability. Many leaders are managing hybrid teams using outdated assumptions from either fully in-office or fully remote environments, resulting in friction, slow decisions, and uneven performance. This Leadercamp helps leaders shift from generic hybrid rules to intentional practices that build clarity, trust, and results. Two times: 9:00 AM - 10:00 AM and 12:00 PM- 1:00 PM.
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Spring 2026 SoE Midterm Student Course Evaluations: Reviewing Evaluation Results (Training for Instructors and TAs) Webinar
item.description: Date: Wednesday, March 18, 2026 12:00 PM - 1:00 PM - This webinar is for instructors and TA’s that would like to understand or have a refresher of the various ways to review their student course evaluations in HelioCampus. This webinar focuses on reviewing midterm course evaluations for instructors in the School of Engineering.
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Faculty and Staff Quick Learn Webinar: Considering Brave Spaces vs. Safe Spaces in Discussions
item.description:
Date: Tuesday, March 24, 2026 12:00 PM - 12:30 PM - In this workshop we will look at the strategies around building brave spaces versus safe spaces and what that means in situations when in a learning environment with a group of peers discussing challenging topics. Maureen Fodera (Office of Undergraduate Education) will be presenting this workshop. Please register using the Event URL in the See section of the calendar event.

For information about sharing your news or events, or to change your subscription settings, visit morningmail.rpi.edu.
item.author: Faculty and Staff
